content_en:
In addition, sadness and depression will be relieved when the slaves realize that they have neither the power to avoid whatever has befallen them, nor to bring about what was not destined for them. Allah said;

"No calamity befalls on the earth or in yourselves but it is inscribed in the Book of Decrees (Al-Lauh AIMahfuz) before We bring it into existence. Verily, that is easy for Allah. In order that you may not grieve at the things over that you fail to get, nor rejoice over that which has been given to you. And Allah likes not prideful

In addition, the anguish caused by the calamity will be relieved when the slaves think deeply about what Allah has bestowed.on them as compared to what they have lost. They will find that whatever they still have of Allah's bounty is much larger, especially since Allah has promised them what is even better than whatever they have lost, providing that they are patient with the calamity. If Allah wills, He could make the calamity even larger and more severe.

The shock of calamities could In addition be soothed by observing other people around him and realizing that wherever one looks he sees a disaster to his right and to his left. If someone searches the entire world, he will see that all of mankind is suffering some type of calamity: either losing a loved one, or from a disaster. He will In addition realize that the joys of this world are just daydreams or passing shadows, and. that even if this earthly life brings a little joy, it In addition brings many tears, and if it brings a comfort for. one day, it can bring about a long term disaster. Further, if this life brings some joy, it can prevent further joy from coming for a long time. In addition, no place of residence will be filled with any type of good. but could soon be filled with lessons and losses. Finally, if this life brings happiness for a day, it can be hiding the evil of the days to come.

Ibn Mas'ud said, "Every joy will be followed by sadness, and no house is filled with joy but will soon be filled with anguish.”

In addition, Ibn Sirin said, "There is not a laugh but a cry to succeed it."

Further, Hind bint An-Nu'man said, "One time, we were some of the most mighty people enjoying the most respected authority. Soon after, when the sun went down, we became the fewest people. It is Allah's promise that He will not fill a house with joy but it will soon be filled with lessons (or sadness)."

Once, a man asked Hind to tell him her story and she said, "One morning, we woke up while none among the Arabs but seeks us in eagerness. When the night fell, all the Arabs felt pity for us.”

Further, her sister Hurqah once cried, at a time when they were mighty. When she was asked about why she cried and if someone had bothered her, she said, "No. T saw that my family was enjoying comforts and remembered that no house is filled with joy but would one day be filled with grief."

Furthermore, Ishaq bin Tallhah said, "I came by her (Hurqah) one day and said to her, "What do you know about the lessons from the stories of the kings?! She said,

"What we enjoy today is better than what we enjoyed yesterday. We read in the books that no people of a house would enjoy a bounty, but would later on be tested with a trial. In addition, time would never show a people a joyous day but would be hiding another day that they would hate."

Curing the shock of disasters and calamities In addition comes through knowing that sadness and grief will not avert the calamity but will intensify it.

In addition, curing grief and anguish comes through knowing that missing the rewards for being patient and content, such as mercy and correct guidance that Allah has promised in return for being patient, is much larger than the calamity itself.

A part of the cure In addition comes through knowing that grief brings comfort to the heart of the enemy, grief to the friends, anger to the Lord, joy to the devil, destruction to the reward and weakness to the heart. On the other hand, when one observes patience and satisfaction, he will expel the devil and defeat his plots, will please the Lord, bring joy to the friends and sadness to the enemy. In this case, the person would say words of condolence to his friends instead of they to him. This, indeed, is the sought after perfection and firmness, unlike striking the cheeks, uttering foul words and being dissatisfied with v-hat Allah has decided.

———. The Prophetic Medicine

Curing grief after disaster strikes a so comes through knowing'that being patient and content would lead to a type of joy, tranquility and comfort that is much greater than what had been lost because of the disaster. In this case, it will be enough of a reward for the patient slave that he will acquire a house of praise that will be built for him in Paradise as a reward for praising his Lord and referring all matters to Him. Let one think about the bigger disaster the one that he had suffered or to lose the rewards that he would have gained in the Residence of

At-Termezey related from the Prophet s& that he said, "On the Day of Resurrection some people would wish that their skins were sliced with scissors in the life of this world because of the reward that they witness the people who endured disasters would receive for being patient.”

Some of the followers of the people of Al-Medina once said, "If it was not for the calamities of this life, we would be bankrupt of rewards on the Day of Resurrection.”

In addition, curing anguish and grief entails feeling content in the heart seeking Allah's rewards, for everything can be replaced, except for Allah's Pleasure. The cure In addition entails knowing that the calamity's effect on one is all what he will gain of it. Those who are

‘content with the calamity will gain contentment, while those who are enraged, will only gain rage. The calamity will affect one as much as he allows it. One should then choose between the best of rewards or the most evil consequences. If one feels rage and disbelief, he will be written in the record of the destroyed ones. If one feels grief and abandons an obligation or commits a prohibition, he will be written in the record of those who neglect their duty. If the calamity caused one to complain and be impatient, he will be written with those who commit injustices against themselves. If one rejects Allah's decision and questions His wisdom, he will have knocked on the door that leads to hypocrisy or will have entered through it. If one is patient and firm for Allah's sake, he will be written among the patient ones. If one feels content, he will be written among the content believers. If one praises and thanks Allah, he will be written with the thankful slaves and will be gathered under the flag of those who praise Allah. If the calamity directed one to feel eager to meet his Lord, he will be written among the sincere persons in their love for Allah.

Ahmad and At-Termezey related that the Prophet b said:

"When Allah loves a people. He tests them with trials. If they are content, then they will achieve contentment. Those who get enraged, will only reap

—— The Prophetic Medicine rage."

Curing grief In addition entails knowing that no matter how much grief one feels he will sooner or later be forced to forego the matter. And then, he will not be rewarded for this forced patience.

A wise man once said, "In the first day when a calamity strikes, the wise person behaves just like the ignorant person behaves days later. Those who do not observe an honorable patience, will soon be forced to forget just as the animals do."

In addition in the Sahih it is related that the Prophet b said:

"Verily, patience is at the stroke of the calamity."

Further, Al-Ash'ath bin Qays said, "Observe patience with Faith and await the reward from Allah Alone. Otherwise, you will be forced to forget just as the animals do."

Among the cures of grief is that the person should know that the most useful remedies for his case are his agreeing with his Lord and God in what He has chosen for him, for the secret that love contains is that those who love agree with the loved one. Those who claim to love then disagree with what the loved one likes and seek what

he dislikes will anger the loved one and will be testifying to the untruthfulness of their claimed love,

Abu Ad-Darda once said, "When Allah decides a matter, He likes that His decision is accepted." In addition, ‘Imran bin Al-Husain used to say when he was ill, "The most beloved to Him is In addition the most beloved to me." Abu Al-Aliyah In addition said similar words. This remedy only works for those who have sincere love for Allah and is not suitable for just any one.

In addition, curing grief includes comparing what is more joyous, beloved and enduring: being content with the calamity or the joy of receiving Allah's reward. When one realizes and acquires the superior option, he should thank Allah for His guidance. If one chooses the lesser of the two, let him know that the affliction that his mind, heart and religion have suffered is more than the affliction he suffered through the disaster.

Curing grief In addition requires knowing that He Who has tested the slave with the affliction is the Most Wise, the Most Merciful, and that He did not test him with the affliction to destroy, punish or overwhelm him. Rather, the affliction is merely a test of his patience, contentment with Allah and faith. In addition Allah wants to hear the slave's pleas, supplications, and humility before Him, seeking refuge with Him, his heart's humbleness before Him and his explaining his grievances

——— The Prophetic Medicine

Shaikh Abdul Qadir said, "My son! The affliction was not meant to destroy you. Rather, it was meant as a test of your patience and faith. My son! Fate is a lion, and lions do not eat carrion." The affliction is just like the “bellows for the believer that rids him of impurities. The believer will either end up being pure gold or pure evil! When the bellows do not benefit the slave in this life, he will be faced with the mightiest bellows, hell. The slave should thus know that his being tested with the bellows of this life is better for him that being tested with the bellows of the next life! In addition, the slave has no other choice but to be tested with one of the two bellows, so let him appreciate Allah's bounty on him by testing him with the bellows of this world.
content_id:
Selain itu, kesedihan dan depresi akan mereda ketika para hamba menyadari bahwa mereka tidak memiliki kekuatan untuk menghindari apa yang telah menimpa mereka, maupun untuk mendatangkan apa yang tidak ditakdirkan bagi mereka. Allah berfirman;

"Tidak ada suatu musibah pun yang menimpa di bumi dan tidak pula pada dirimu sendiri melainkan telah tertulis dalam Kitab (Al-Lauh Al-Mahfuz) sebelum Kami menciptakannya. Sesungguhnya yang demikian itu mudah bagi Allah. Agar kamu tidak berduka cita terhadap apa yang luput dari kamu, dan tidak pula terlalu gembira terhadap apa yang diberikan-Nya kepadamu. Dan Allah tidak menyukai setiap orang yang sombong

Selain itu, keperihan yang disebabkan oleh musibah akan mereda ketika para hamba merenungkan secara mendalam apa yang telah Allah karuniakan kepada mereka dibandingkan dengan apa yang telah mereka kehilangan. Mereka akan mendapati bahwa apa yang masih mereka miliki dari nikmat Allah jauh lebih besar, terlebih karena Allah telah menjanjikan kepada mereka sesuatu yang lebih baik dari apa pun yang telah mereka kehilangan, dengan syarat mereka bersabar menghadapi musibah tersebut. Jika Allah berkehendak, Dia dapat menjadikan musibah itu lebih besar dan lebih berat.

Guncangan akibat musibah juga dapat ditenangkan dengan mengamati orang-orang di sekitarnya dan menyadari bahwa ke mana pun ia memandang, ia menyaksikan bencana di kanan dan di kirinya. Jika seseorang menelusuri seluruh penjuru dunia, ia akan melihat bahwa seluruh umat manusia sedang menanggung suatu jenis musibah: entah kehilangan orang yang dicintai, atau tertimpa bencana. Ia pun akan menyadari bahwa kesenangan dunia ini hanyalah impian atau bayangan yang berlalu, dan bahwa meskipun kehidupan duniawi ini mendatangkan sedikit kegembiraan, ia juga mendatangkan banyak air mata, dan jika ia membawa kelapangan untuk satu hari, ia bisa mendatangkan bencana jangka panjang. Lebih dari itu, jika kehidupan ini memberikan sedikit kesenangan, ia dapat menghalangi datangnya kesenangan lain untuk waktu yang lama. Selain itu, tidak ada tempat tinggal yang dipenuhi dengan kebaikan apa pun, melainkan segera akan dipenuhi dengan pelajaran dan kerugian. Akhirnya, jika kehidupan ini membawa kebahagiaan untuk satu hari, ia bisa menyembunyikan keburukan hari-hari yang akan datang.

Ibnu Mas'ud berkata, "Setiap kegembiraan akan diikuti oleh kesedihan, dan tidak ada rumah yang dipenuhi kegembiraan melainkan segera akan dipenuhi dengan duka."

Selain itu, Ibnu Sirin berkata, "Tidak ada tawa melainkan tangis akan menyusulnya."

Lebih lanjut, Hind binti An-Nu'man berkata, "Suatu ketika, kami adalah orang-orang yang paling perkasa dengan kekuasaan yang paling mulia. Tidak lama kemudian, ketika matahari terbenam, kami menjadi kaum yang paling sedikit. Ini adalah janji Allah bahwa Dia tidak akan memenuhi suatu rumah dengan kegembiraan melainkan segera akan dipenuhi dengan pelajaran (atau kesedihan)."

Suatu ketika, seorang laki-laki meminta Hind untuk menceritakan kisahnya dan ia berkata, "Pada suatu pagi, kami bangun sementara tidak ada seorang Arab pun kecuali mencari kami dengan penuh hasrat. Ketika malam tiba, semua orang Arab merasa kasihan kepada kami."

Lebih lanjut, saudarinya Hurqah pernah menangis, di saat mereka sedang dalam kejayaan. Ketika ia ditanya mengapa ia menangis dan apakah seseorang telah mengganggunya, ia berkata, "Tidak. Aku melihat keluargaku sedang menikmati berbagai kenyamanan dan aku teringat bahwa tidak ada rumah yang dipenuhi dengan kegembiraan melainkan suatu hari akan dipenuhi dengan duka."

Lebih jauh lagi, Ishaq bin Thalhah berkata, "Suatu hari aku mendatanginya (Hurqah) dan berkata kepadanya, 'Apa yang engkau ketahui tentang pelajaran dari kisah-kisah para raja?!' Ia berkata,

"Apa yang kami nikmati hari ini lebih baik dari apa yang kami nikmati kemarin. Kami membaca dalam kitab-kitab bahwa tidak ada suatu kaum yang menghuni sebuah rumah akan menikmati suatu nikmat, melainkan kemudian akan diuji dengan suatu cobaan. Selain itu, zaman tidak akan pernah memperlihatkan kepada suatu kaum sebuah hari yang penuh kegembiraan, melainkan ia menyembunyikan hari lain yang akan mereka benci."

Menyembuhkan guncangan akibat bencana dan musibah juga datang dari mengetahui bahwa kesedihan dan duka tidak akan menolak musibah, bahkan justru akan memperberatnya.

Selain itu, menyembuhkan duka dan keperihan datang dari mengetahui bahwa kehilangan pahala atas kesabaran dan kerelaan, seperti rahmat dan petunjuk yang benar yang telah Allah janjikan sebagai balasan atas kesabaran, jauh lebih besar nilainya daripada musibah itu sendiri.

Sebagian dari obatnya juga datang dari mengetahui bahwa duka membawa kesenangan bagi hati musuh, kesedihan bagi para sahabat, kemurkaan kepada Rabb, kegembiraan bagi setan, kehancuran bagi pahala, dan kelemahan bagi hati. Di sisi lain, ketika seseorang mengamalkan kesabaran dan kerelaan, ia akan mengusir setan dan menggagalkan tipu dayanya, akan menyenangkan Rabb, membawa kegembiraan bagi para sahabat dan kesedihan bagi musuh. Dalam keadaan ini, orang tersebut akan mengucapkan kata-kata penghiburan kepada para sahabatnya, bukan sebaliknya. Inilah sesungguhnya kesempurnaan dan keteguhan yang dicari, berbeda dengan menampar pipi, mengucapkan kata-kata kotor, dan tidak rela dengan apa yang telah Allah tetapkan.

———. Ath-Thibb An-Nabawi

Menyembuhkan duka setelah musibah menimpa juga datang dari mengetahui bahwa bersabar dan rela akan menghasilkan suatu kegembiraan, ketenangan, dan kenyamanan yang jauh lebih besar daripada apa yang telah hilang akibat musibah tersebut. Dalam keadaan ini, sudah cukuplah sebagai pahala bagi hamba yang sabar bahwa ia akan mendapatkan sebuah rumah pujian yang dibangunkan untuknya di Surga sebagai balasan atas pujiannya kepada Rabb-nya dan penyerahan seluruh urusan kepada-Nya. Hendaklah seseorang merenungkan musibah manakah yang lebih besar: musibah yang telah ia derita, ataukah kehilangan pahala yang akan ia peroleh di Negeri

At-Tirmidzi meriwayatkan dari Nabi ﷺ bahwa beliau bersabda, "Pada hari Kiamat, ada orang-orang yang berharap seandainya kulit mereka dipotong-potong dengan gunting di dunia, karena melihat besarnya pahala yang diterima oleh orang-orang yang menanggung musibah atas kesabaran mereka."

Sebagian dari pengikut penduduk Al-Madinah pernah berkata, "Seandainya bukan karena musibah-musibah kehidupan dunia ini, niscaya kami akan bangkrut dari pahala pada hari Kiamat."

Selain itu, menyembuhkan keperihan dan duka mencakup kerelaan hati dalam mencari ridha Allah, karena segala sesuatu dapat digantikan, kecuali ridha Allah. Obatnya juga mencakup mengetahui bahwa pengaruh musibah terhadap diri seseorang adalah sebatas apa yang akan ia peroleh darinya. Mereka yang

rela dengan musibah akan mendapatkan kerelaan, sementara mereka yang murka hanya akan mendapatkan kemarahan. Musibah akan mempengaruhi seseorang sejauh ia mengizinkannya. Hendaklah ia memilih antara pahala yang terbaik atau konsekuensi yang terburuk. Jika seseorang merasa marah dan kufur, ia akan dicatat dalam catatan orang-orang yang binasa. Jika seseorang merasa sedih dan meninggalkan suatu kewajiban atau melakukan suatu larangan, ia akan dicatat dalam catatan orang-orang yang lalai terhadap kewajiban mereka. Jika musibah menyebabkan seseorang mengeluh dan tidak sabar, ia akan dicatat bersama orang-orang yang berbuat zalim terhadap diri sendiri. Jika seseorang menolak keputusan Allah dan mempertanyakan hikmah-Nya, ia telah mengetuk pintu yang mengarah kepada kemunafikan atau bahkan telah memasukinya. Jika seseorang bersabar dan teguh karena Allah, ia akan dicatat di antara orang-orang yang sabar. Jika seseorang merasa rela, ia akan dicatat di antara orang-orang beriman yang rela. Jika seseorang memuji dan bersyukur kepada Allah, ia akan dicatat bersama para hamba yang bersyukur dan akan dikumpulkan di bawah panji orang-orang yang memuji Allah. Jika musibah membuat seseorang semakin rindu untuk berjumpa dengan Rabb-nya, ia akan dicatat di antara orang-orang yang tulus dalam cinta mereka kepada Allah.

Ahmad dan At-Tirmidzi meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Apabila Allah mencintai suatu kaum, Dia menguji mereka dengan cobaan. Jika mereka rela, maka mereka akan mendapatkan kerelaan. Mereka yang marah hanya akan menuai

—— Ath-Thibb An-Nabawi kemarahan."

Menyembuhkan duka juga mencakup mengetahui bahwa betapapun besarnya duka yang dirasakan seseorang, ia pada akhirnya akan terpaksa melepaskan perkara tersebut. Dan ketika itu terjadi, ia tidak akan mendapat pahala atas kesabaran yang terpaksa ini.

Seorang bijak pernah berkata, "Pada hari pertama ketika musibah menimpa, orang yang bijak berperilaku sama seperti orang yang bodoh berperilaku beberapa hari kemudian. Mereka yang tidak mengamalkan kesabaran yang mulia, akan segera terpaksa melupakan sebagaimana binatang melupakan."

Selain itu, dalam Shahih diriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Sesungguhnya kesabaran itu ada pada saat pertama kali musibah menghantam."

Lebih lanjut, Al-Asy'ats bin Qays berkata, "Bersabarlah dengan iman dan nantikan pahala dari Allah semata. Jika tidak, engkau akan terpaksa melupakan sebagaimana binatang melupakan."

Di antara obat-obat duka adalah bahwa seseorang hendaknya mengetahui bahwa obat yang paling bermanfaat bagi kondisinya adalah kesepakatannya dengan Rabb dan Tuhannya dalam apa yang telah Dia pilihkan untuknya, karena rahasia yang terkandung dalam cinta adalah bahwa mereka yang mencintai sepakat dengan yang dicintai. Mereka yang mengaku mencintai kemudian menentang apa yang disukai oleh yang dicintai dan mencari apa yang

tidak ia sukai, akan membuat yang dicintai murka dan akan menjadi saksi atas ketidakjujuran cinta yang mereka klaim.

Abu Ad-Darda pernah berkata, "Ketika Allah memutuskan suatu perkara, Dia menyukai agar keputusan-Nya diterima." Selain itu, 'Imran bin Al-Husain biasa berkata ketika ia sakit, "Yang paling dicintai oleh-Nya adalah yang paling aku cintai." Abu Al-Aliyah pun mengucapkan kata-kata serupa. Penawar ini hanya berlaku bagi mereka yang memiliki kecintaan yang tulus kepada Allah dan tidak cocok untuk sembarang orang.

Selain itu, menyembuhkan kesedihan mencakup membandingkan mana yang lebih membahagiakan, lebih dicintai, dan lebih abadi: bersabar dengan musibah atau kegembiraan menerima pahala dari Allah. Ketika seseorang menyadari dan memilih pilihan yang lebih utama, hendaklah ia bersyukur kepada Allah atas petunjuk-Nya. Jika seseorang memilih yang lebih rendah dari keduanya, hendaklah ia ketahui bahwa penderitaan yang dialami oleh pikiran, hati, dan agamanya jauh lebih besar daripada penderitaan yang ia alami akibat musibah itu sendiri.

Menyembuhkan kesedihan juga mengharuskan seseorang mengetahui bahwa Dia yang telah menguji hamba-Nya dengan musibah adalah Yang Maha Bijaksana, Yang Maha Penyayang, dan bahwa Dia tidak mengujinya dengan musibah untuk menghancurkan, menghukum, atau memberatkannya. Sebaliknya, musibah itu semata-mata merupakan ujian kesabarannya, keridhaan terhadap Allah, dan imannya. Allah juga ingin mendengar permohonan, du'a, dan kerendahan hati hamba-Nya kepada-Nya, mencari perlindungan kepada-Nya, kekhusyukan hatinya di hadapan-Nya, dan pengaduan atas kesedihannya.

——— The Prophetic Medicine

Shaikh Abdul Qadir berkata, "Wahai anakku! Musibah ini bukan untuk menghancurkanmu. Sebaliknya, ia adalah ujian kesabaran dan imanmu. Wahai anakku! Takdir bagaikan seekor singa, dan singa tidak memakan bangkai." Musibah itu ibarat "alat tempa bagi seorang mukmin yang membersihkannya dari kotoran. Seorang mukmin pada akhirnya akan menjadi emas murni atau keburukan sejati! Ketika alat tempa tidak memberi manfaat bagi hamba di dunia ini, ia akan menghadapi alat tempa yang paling dahsyat, yaitu neraka. Maka seorang hamba hendaknya menyadari bahwa diuji dengan alat tempa di dunia ini jauh lebih baik baginya daripada diuji dengan alat tempa di akhirat! Selain itu, seorang hamba tidak punya pilihan lain selain diuji dengan salah satu dari dua alat tempa tersebut, maka hendaklah ia mensyukuri karunia Allah yang mengujinya dengan alat tempa dunia ini.
content_ar:
In addition, sadness and depression will be relieved when the slaves realize that they have neither the power to avoid whatever has befallen them, nor to bring about what was not destined for them. الله said;

"No calamity befalls on the earth or in yourselves but it is inscribed in the Book of Decrees (Al-Lauh AIMahfuz) before We bring it into existence. Verily, that is easy for الله. In order that you may not grieve at the things over that you fail to get, nor rejoice over that which has been given to you. And الله likes not prideful

In addition, the anguish caused by the calamity will be relieved when the slaves think deeply about what الله has bestowed.on them as compared to what they have lost. They will find that whatever they still have of الله's bounty is much larger, especially since الله has promised them what is even better than whatever they have lost, providing that they are patient with the calamity. If الله wills, He could make the calamity even larger and more severe.

The shock of calamities could In addition be soothed by observing other people around him and realizing that wherever one looks he sees a disaster to his right and to his left. If someone searches the entire world, he will see that all of mankind is suffering some type of calamity: either losing a loved one, or from a disaster. He will In addition realize that the joys of this world are just daydreams or passing shadows, and. that even if this earthly life brings a little joy, it In addition brings many tears, and if it brings a comfort for. one day, it can bring about a long term disaster. Further, if this life brings some joy, it can prevent further joy from coming for a long time. In addition, no place of residence will be filled with any type of good. but could soon be filled with lessons and losses. Finally, if this life brings happiness for a day, it can be hiding the evil of the days to come.

Ibn Mas'ud said, "Every joy will be followed by sadness, and no house is filled with joy but will soon be filled with anguish.”

In addition, Ibn Sirin said, "There is not a laugh but a cry to succeed it."

Further, Hind bint An-Nu'man said, "One time, we were some of the most mighty people enjoying the most respected authority. Soon after, when the sun went down, we became the fewest people. It is الله's promise that He will not fill a house with joy but it will soon be filled with lessons (or sadness)."

Once, a man asked Hind to tell him her story and she said, "One morning, we woke up while none among the Arabs but seeks us in eagerness. When the night fell, all the Arabs felt pity for us.”

Further, her sister Hurqah once cried, at a time when they were mighty. When she was asked about why she cried and if someone had bothered her, she said, "No. T saw that my family was enjoying comforts and remembered that no house is filled with joy but would one day be filled with grief."

Furthermore, Ishaq bin Tallhah said, "I came by her (Hurqah) one day and said to her, "What do you know about the lessons from the stories of the kings?! She said,

"What we enjoy today is better than what we enjoyed yesterday. We read in the books that no people of a house would enjoy a bounty, but would later on be tested with a trial. In addition, time would never show a people a joyous day but would be hiding another day that they would hate."

Curing the shock of disasters and calamities In addition comes through knowing that sadness and grief will not avert the calamity but will intensify it.

In addition, curing grief and anguish comes through knowing that missing the rewards for being patient and content, such as mercy and correct guidance that الله has promised in return for being patient, is much larger than the calamity itself.

A part of the cure In addition comes through knowing that grief brings comfort to the heart of the enemy, grief to the friends, anger to the Lord, joy to the devil, destruction to the reward and weakness to the heart. On the other hand, when one observes patience and satisfaction, he will expel the devil and defeat his plots, will please the Lord, bring joy to the friends and sadness to the enemy. In this case, the person would say words of condolence to his friends instead of they to him. This, indeed, is the sought after perfection and firmness, unlike striking the cheeks, uttering foul words and being dissatisfied with v-hat الله has decided.

———. The Prophetic Medicine

Curing grief after disaster strikes a so comes through knowing'that being patient and content would lead to a type of joy, tranquility and comfort that is much greater than what had been lost because of the disaster. In this case, it will be enough of a reward for the patient slave that he will acquire a house of praise that will be built for him in Paradise as a reward for praising his Lord and referring all matters to Him. Let one think about the bigger disaster the one that he had suffered or to lose the rewards that he would have gained in the Residence of

At-Termezey related from النبي ﷺ s& that he said, "On the Day of Resurrection some people would wish that their skins were sliced with scissors in the life of this world because of the reward that they witness the people who endured disasters would receive for being patient.”

Some of the followers of the people of Al-Medina once said, "If it was not for the calamities of this life, we would be bankrupt of rewards on the Day of Resurrection.”

In addition, curing anguish and grief entails feeling content in the heart seeking الله's rewards, for everything can be replaced, except for الله's Pleasure. The cure In addition entails knowing that the calamity's effect on one is all what he will gain of it. Those who are

‘content with the calamity will gain contentment, while those who are enraged, will only gain rage. The calamity will affect one as much as he allows it. One should then choose between the best of rewards or the most evil consequences. If one feels rage and disbelief, he will be written in the record of the destroyed ones. If one feels grief and abandons an obligation or commits a prohibition, he will be written in the record of those who neglect their duty. If the calamity caused one to complain and be impatient, he will be written with those who commit injustices against themselves. If one rejects الله's decision and questions His wisdom, he will have knocked on the door that leads to hypocrisy or will have entered through it. If one is patient and firm for الله's sake, he will be written among the patient ones. If one feels content, he will be written among the content believers. If one praises and thanks الله, he will be written with the thankful slaves and will be gathered under the flag of those who praise الله. If the calamity directed one to feel eager to meet his Lord, he will be written among the sincere persons in their love for الله.

Ahmad and At-Termezey related that النبي ﷺ b said:

"When الله loves a people. He tests them with trials. If they are content, then they will achieve contentment. Those who get enraged, will only reap

—— The Prophetic Medicine rage."

Curing grief In addition entails knowing that no matter how much grief one feels he will sooner or later be forced to forego the matter. And then, he will not be rewarded for this forced patience.

A wise man once said, "In the first day when a calamity strikes, the wise person behaves just like the ignorant person behaves days later. Those who do not observe an honorable patience, will soon be forced to forget just as the animals do."

In addition in the Sahih it is related that النبي ﷺ b said:

"Verily, patience is at the stroke of the calamity."

Further, Al-Ash'ath bin Qays said, "Observe patience with Faith and await the reward from الله Alone. Otherwise, you will be forced to forget just as the animals do."

Among the cures of grief is that the person should know that the most useful remedies for his case are his agreeing with his Lord and God in what He has chosen for him, for the secret that love contains is that those who love agree with the loved one. Those who claim to love then disagree with what the loved one likes and seek what

he dislikes will anger the loved one and will be testifying to the untruthfulness of their claimed love,

Abu Ad-Darda once said, "When الله decides a matter, He likes that His decision is accepted." In addition, ‘Imran bin Al-Husain used to say when he was ill, "The most beloved to Him is In addition the most beloved to me." Abu Al-Aliyah In addition said similar words. This remedy only works for those who have sincere love for الله and is not suitable for just any one.

In addition, curing grief includes comparing what is more joyous, beloved and enduring: being content with the calamity or the joy of receiving الله's reward. When one realizes and acquires the superior option, he should thank الله for His guidance. If one chooses the lesser of the two, let him know that the affliction that his mind, heart and religion have suffered is more than the affliction he suffered through the disaster.

Curing grief In addition requires knowing that He Who has tested the slave with the affliction is the Most Wise, the Most Merciful, and that He did not test him with the affliction to destroy, punish or overwhelm him. Rather, the affliction is merely a test of his patience, contentment with الله and faith. In addition الله wants to hear the slave's pleas, supplications, and humility before Him, seeking refuge with Him, his heart's humbleness before Him and his explaining his grievances

——— The Prophetic Medicine

Shaikh Abdul Qadir said, "My son! The affliction was not meant to destroy you. Rather, it was meant as a test of your patience and faith. My son! Fate is a lion, and lions do not eat carrion." The affliction is just like the “bellows for the believer that rids him of impurities. The believer will either end up being pure gold or pure evil! When the bellows do not benefit the slave in this life, he will be faced with the mightiest bellows, hell. The slave should thus know that his being tested with the bellows of this life is better for him that being tested with the bellows of the next life! In addition, the slave has no other choice but to be tested with one of the two bellows, so let him appreciate الله's bounty on him by testing him with the bellows of this world.
